channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Jan. 6: Security preparations in DC on anniversary of Capitol Riots
item.description: Jan. 6, 2025, marks four years since the Capitol riots when former President Donald Trump denied the election results.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: A Melania Trump documentary from director Brett Ratner will be released by Amazon
item.description: Incoming first lady Melania Trump will be the subject of a new documentary directed by Brett Ratner and distributed by Amazon Prime Video.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Border shelters relieved the pressure during migrant surges. Under Trump, they could become a target
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — When Roselins Sequera's family of seven finally reached the U.S. from Venezuela, they spent weeks at a migrant shelter on the Texas border that gave them a place to sleep, meals and tips for finding work.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Higher Social Security payments coming for millions of people from bill that Biden signed
item.description: President Joe Biden has signed into law a measure that boosts Social Security payments for current and former public employees.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No Jan. 6 disruptions are expected as Trump's win boosts Republicans' faith in elections -- for now
item.description: Four years ago, then-President Donald Trump rallied his supporters to march to the U.S. Capitol and urged his vice president to stop the congressional certification of Democrat Joe Biden's victory.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump appears with Italian Prime Minister Meloni at his Florida club
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump made an appearance with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, who was visiting his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden's decision to block Nippon Steel takeover creates uncertainty for U.S. Steel workers
item.description: By blocking a Japanese company’s takeover of U.S. Steel, President Joe Biden says he is protecting good jobs in the American heartland.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman And Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jill Biden gets the priciest gift from a foreign leader in 2023
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamo
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected House speaker in dramatic floor vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Mike Johnson narrowly won reelection Friday as House speaker on a first ballot, overcoming hard-right GOP holdouts in a tense standoff and buoyed by a nod of support from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress is notified by the Biden administration of a planned $8 billion weapons sale to Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department has informed Congress of a planned $8 billion weapons sale to Israel, U.S. officials say, as the American ally presses forward with its war against Hamas in Gaza. Some of the arms in the package could be sent through current U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul seeks expanded involuntary commitment laws over violent crimes on subway
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul wants to expand the state’s involuntary commitment laws to allow hospitals to compel more people with mental health struggles into treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Excerpts from the judge's ruling to go ahead with Trump sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's hush money case in New York dealt with a host of weighty, unprecedented questions before deciding Friday that the president-elect should be sentenced later this month for the crime of falsifying business records.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Johnson re-elected speaker: Here were the top three moments of the dramatic vote
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., narrowly held onto the gavel in a nail-biter vote on Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden blocks $14 billion acquisition of US Steel by Japan's Nippon Steel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has blocked the nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel — affirming his earlier vow on the presidential campaign trail to prevent the acquisition of Steeltown USA’s most storied steel company.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, JOSH BOAK and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Majority Leader Thune kicks off Senate session with pledge to preserve filibuster
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Senate Majority Leader John Thune is kicking off the new session with a promise to preserve the filibuster, saying in his first floor speech as leader that his priority will be to “ensure the Senate stays the Senate.” Thune, R-S.D.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: City of Albuquerque misused federal funds to give bonuses to staff: report
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – A new Office of the Inspector General report accuses Mayor Tim Keller's Administration of misusing COVID-19 federal grant money. A city councilor is now calling for a federal investigation.
item.dc:creator: Gabe Chavez, NewsNation

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: The ruling hands a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden admin officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson battles for the Speakership: 5 things to watch
item.description: Friday’s Speaker vote is shaping up to be a nail-biter as Rep. Mike Johnson (R-La.), the current House leader, is scrambling to keep the gavel for the next two years in the face of conservative critics furious with his leadership style.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Here’s what Congress’s first day will look like
item.description: The 119th Congress kicks off Friday as lawmakers return to Washington with a long January to-do list and a packed first day. Confirmation hearings for President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees are set to get rolling in the coming weeks, and Trump himself will be inaugurated in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: New Year's attacks stoke fears of political violence
item.description: A pair of fatal attacks on New Year's Day have reignited concerns over political violence in the U.S. after an election already marked by multiple assassination attempts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Whip list: Lawmakers to watch in Friday’s Speaker vote
item.description: About a dozen lawmakers remain major question marks in Speaker Mike Johnson's (R-La.) quest to win a floor vote for the gavel Friday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation
